title: one operation per user intent keeps concerns separated
content:
# one operation per user intent keeps concerns separated

Each operation should correspond to a single user action. This keeps operations atomic, testable, and replayable:

```
✅ ADD_LINE_ITEM — adds one line item
✅ UPDATE_RECIPIENT — changes the recipient
✅ SET_DUE_DATE — sets the due date

❌ UPDATE_INVOICE — changes recipient, adds items, and sets date in one operation
```

Descriptive operation names (e.g., `ADD_LINE_ITEM`, `UPDATE_RECIPIENT`) communicate intent better than generic names (e.g., `UPDATE_DATA`).

Since [[operation design uses descriptive names and includes examples and errors]], each operation should also define error types and include examples in the document model.

---

Topics:
- [[reducer patterns]]
- [[schema design]]
description: Operations should map to distinct user actions like ADD_LINE_ITEM rather than batching multiple intents into one operation
